topics.title: Ecological Validity in Neuropsychological Assessment
topics.controversy: The gap between laboratory performance and real-world functional ability.
topics.key_findings: Traditional tests often fail to predict daily life success. Virtual Reality is emerging as a bridge.

topics.title: Digital Biomarkers in Neurodegenerative Diseases
topics.controversy: Clinical validation vs. technological promise and privacy ethics.
topics.key_findings: Passive monitoring can detect decline years earlier, but standardization is lacking.

topics.title: Neuropsychology of ADHD in Adults
topics.controversy: Diagnostic mimics and the necessity of symptom validity testing.
topics.key_findings: High risk of misdiagnosis due to comorbidities; objective deficits are often subtle.

topics.title: Cultural Bias in Standardized Cognitive Testing
topics.controversy: The universal applicability of Western-normed instruments.
topics.key_findings: Education and language proficiency significantly skew results, requiring localized norms.

topics.title: Tele-neuropsychology: Validity and Clinical Utility
topics.controversy: Equivalence to in-person assessment in diverse clinical populations.
topics.key_findings: Meta-analyses support equivalence for most domains; specific motor tests remain a challenge.

topics.title: The Hype of Brain Plasticity in Neurorehabilitation
topics.controversy: Overpromising recovery outcomes in chronic brain injury cases.
topics.key_findings: Neural reorganization has limits; focus is shifting from 'rewiring' to 'compensation'.

topics.title: Malingering Detection in Forensic Neuropsychology
topics.controversy: The ethical and statistical limits of performance validity tests.
topics.key_findings: PVTs are essential in forensics but require cautious interpretation to avoid false positives.

topics.title: Neuroimaging vs. Neuropsychological Testing
topics.controversy: Which provides better predictive value for functional decline?
topics.key_findings: Imaging shows 'where' and 'what', but neuropsychology shows 'how' the patient functions.

topics.title: Neuropsychological Profiles in Long COVID
topics.controversy: Brain fog: Objective impairment or subjective distress?
topics.key_findings: Discrepancy between self-report and testing; executive dysfunction is the most common objective finding.

topics.title: Gender and Sex Differences in Cognitive Aging
topics.controversy: Biological vulnerability vs. sociocultural disadvantage in dementia risk.
topics.key_findings: Women show faster decline in some cohorts, linked to reproductive health and education history.
